history:
In the past players used to be able to summon an unlimited number of creatures including stronger creatures than today, like [[Dragons]] which can no longer be summoned. When the player logged off or disconnected the summons would turn into wild monsters that behaved the same as a normal creature.

In the early days of Tibia, when only [[Antica]] existed, even Paladins were able to summon creatures. When the ability was removed, the old paladins did not lose the spells learned from it. The remaining paladins that could summon creatures lost their spell on [[Updates/10.55|Update 10.55]] when vocation requirement was also applied while casting spells, not just while buying them.

notes:
This spell takes into account the [[Level]] and [[Melee]] ([[Axe]], [[Club]], [[Sword]] or [[Fist Fighting]]) skill level to determine how much damage you will deal to the target. It does only one-fourth of damage to players, from the amount of damage it does to creatures.

The damage is not based upon the melee skill level of the type of weapon you are wielding, but rather on the highest melee skill level of your character. So if you want to effectively use this spell, then the smart play is to always try to use it when in a [[Blood Rage]]. The damage range is wide. For a single loose reference point, a level 350 with 100 skill level will cause between 580 and 880 total damage.

Useful for long-winded single battles, as it can supersede the damage of [[Brutal Strike]] after 5 - 6 turns. Also useful for continuously turning invisible creatures visible again.

notes:
It's extremely handy for [[Mage]]s. Note that mana blocked hits do not increase [[Magic Level]]. While this spell is active, all damage received will be displayed in the Game console as <font color="0000ff" class="tibiatext">'''Blue Text'''</font> and the following icon will appear in your condition bar: [[File:Magic Shield Spell Icon.gif|Magic Shield Icon]].

The Magic Shield only absorbs a limited amount of damage before breaking. When this mana is depleted the spell effect disappears. The amount of mana it absorbs depends on the character's [[Level]] and [[Magic Level]], including equipment and skill boosts active at the moment the spell is cast, according to this formula (rounded up to the next whole number), where L is your level and M is your magic level:

<math>Magic Shield Capacity = 7M + 7.6L + \max\{300, 0.4L \}</math>

If you have unlocked the Enhanced Magic Shield Conviction Perk on the Sorcerers' [[Wheel of Destiny]], then the formula is:

<math>Magic Shield Capacity = 8M + 8.6L + \max\{300, 0.4L \}</math>

Recasting the spell restores the shield to its maximum value. While the spell is on cooldown, mages can renew the shield by using a [[Magic Shield Potion]]. The Magic Shield can be removed by casting the [[Cancel Magic Shield]] spell.

Furthermore, with this update the spell cooldown was increased from 2 to 14 seconds while its duration was reduced from 200 to 60 seconds. On the following week, with [[Updates/12.60.10468|Update 12.60.10468]], the Magic Shield duration was increased to 180 seconds.

notes:
[[File:Levitate on entrance.jpg|thumb|The red squares shows where to stand when levitating up beside entrances.]] Very useful spell. Instead of using a stack of [[Parcel]]s, you can use this spell to climb onto ledges or houses. Advantages of this spell include no need to carry parcels and a greater ability to go up in certain places! In an archway, you need to stand on 3 [[parcel]]s and have something blocking you from walking underneath the archway instead of on top of it. Levitate does not have this issue.
'''Warning note''': In some places you can levitate up or down after cuting grass but if the grass grows back again you won't be able to levitate over it, and you will end up trapped. Try the spell on flat walls or in front of doorway openings. The caster must be facing the cliff, wall or entrance for this spell to work. The caster cannot move up or down more than one level at a time. If this spell is cast and the character is not in the correct square to levitate, then no mana is lost.

notes:
If an attack were to kill you but the overkill damage amounts to less than a certain percentage of your maximum [[HP|health]], you will first heal yourself for that same percentage of hit points and only afterwards will the damage be applied. In addition, all your [[spell]] [[cooldown]]s are reduced by 60 seconds. Attacks that deal [[Agony Damage|agony damage]] do not trigger this perk. It is technically not a spell, is casted automatically when the effect triggers, and costs no mana.

The cooldown of this perk is only reduced while the character has this perk available and has a [[Logout Block|battle sign]]. The cooldown timer will stop going down immediately when the battle sign is lost, either because one minute passed since it was triggered or because the character has entered a [[Protection Zone]]. The current cooldown can be checked by adding Gift of Life to an [[Action Bars|action button]] (right click and select "Assign Passive Ability").

notes:
This spell is similar to the Royal Paladin's [[Divine Dazzle]] with the difference that it challenges affected ranged creatures. It is useful in team hunts to avoid distance fighter creatures from targeting the rest of the team, as well as preventing creatures from running in low health, making it also useful for knights when solo-hunting this type of creature.

It's important to note that it will only hit up to 3 creatures, whereas [[Challenge]] can hit up to 8. Furthermore, it cannot be used in rooms of [[Lever Bosses]] to prevent boss mechanics from becoming easier than originally intended. These two factors mean that Knights should still have [[Challenge]] ready to be used even after they acquire Chivalrous Challenge.

If a creature's path to the caster is blocked, creatures might change their target before the spell's duration is over.

Summoned creatures are unaffected by this spell as they will attack what their summoner targets. Players are not affected by this spell.

notes: This spell increases the distance skill of the paladin by 40% of his unmodified distance skill. That is, 40% of the distance skill if there are no boosts obtained from spells, equipment or other items. The increase rounds up if the unmodified distance skill is an odd number. This spell applies a paralysis on the paladin, and a 10 second cooldown to both the ''healing'' and ''support'' channels, preventing the player from healing himself or dispelling the paralysis with [[Haste]]. Other players can still heal the paladin to dispel his paralysis, but they cannot affect the cooldowns. Equipment bonuses stack with Sharpshooter's effects, but the gains from Sharpshooter are lost if another magical buff is applied to your distance, such as distance reduction from monsters or from [[Bullseye Potion]]s. Likewise, if a buff is already active, whether positive or negative, it will be overwritten by Sharpshooter, and even if Sharpshooter wears off, the previous buff will not be reapplied.
history: This spell's skill boost was reduced from 50% to 40% with the [[Updates/12.55|Vocation Adjustments Update]]. Prior to the same update, this spell could not be learned simultaneously with [[Swift Foot]], but if they have both been learned before, the paladin could switch them for a fee of 500 gp at [[Zoltan]].
effect: Increases the [[Distance Fighting|distance skill]] of the [[paladin]] by 40% for 10 seconds. While the spell is in effect, the paladin's speed is reduced by 70%, they cannot cast healing, support or supply spells.
name: Sharpshooter

notes:
This spell dealt a lot of damage over a large area surrounding the caster. This spell was dangerous in [[PvP]] worlds, because it had such a large area of effect and could easily lead to a [[Red Skull]] or a [[Banishment]]. Caused damage on a total of 73 squares (including the one character is standing on).

Depending upon the caster's [[Magic Level]] and [[Level]], this spell could kill strong creatures in one strike.

This spell was replaced by [[Hell's Core]] and [[Rage of the Skies]] spells in the [[Updates/8.1|Winter Update 2007]].
history:
One of the older spells, back in the days you could get it by using the corresponding spell scroll. It only required 800 [[mana]] to cast until the [[Updates/7.6|Winter Update 2005]]. It was the most powerful spell until the [[Updates/8.1|Winter Update 2007]].
|cooldown=1-2s
effect: [[File:Physical Damage Icon.gif]] Makes a big explosion around the caster.
name: Ultimate Explosion

notes: Very useful spell because it heals more hp than [[Exura Ico]], although the drawback is the healing isn't instant.
effect: A healing effect in intervals over time. that heals 20 health points every 3 seconds for 60 seconds (total of 400 health points).
name: Recovery

effect: Stops the [[Special Condition]] called [[Electrified]].
name: Cure Electrification

notes:
This spell is very similar to the Knight's [[Chivalrous Challenge]], however, it will '''not''' make the creatures change their target.

This spell cannot be used in rooms of [[Lever Bosses]] to prevent boss mechanics from becoming easier than originally intended.
effect: [[File:Chivalrous Challenge Icon.png|link=]] Chains up to 3 creatures prioritising ranged creatures in a 6 sqm range. Creatures hit are turned into melee fighters for 8 seconds.
name: Divine Dazzle

notes: This spell takes into account the [[Level]] and [[Magic Level]] to determine how much damage it will deal to the target. The damage is a series of constant values, where each value is followed by a 20% higher next value. The damage is the same for both creatures and players. The last damage value, the final dreadful peak, is calculated by multiplying the last damage value with a percentage ranging from 1 to 120%.
effect: Inflicts [[cursed]] conditions on the target.
name: Curse

notes: This spell belongs to the [[Special Spells]] secondary group, therefore it shares its cooldown with [[Strong Terra Strike]].
effect: [[File:Freezing Icon.gif]] Shoots a powerful [[Ice Damage|ice]] missile at the selected target up to 3 square meters away. If no target is selected, the damage is focused on the single square right in front of the caster.
name: Strong Ice Strike

notes: Can be used as a mage's primary healing spell until their [[HP]] pool increases. When cast by a high level mage, it restores about 50% more health than [[Light Healing]].
effect: Restores a medium amount of [[HP|health]] and cures [[paralysis]].
name: Intense Healing

notes: This spell is meant to be used primarily as a finishing move because of the cost and the fact that casting it puts '''ALL''' combat spells on a 4-second cooldown. Considering that the spell's damage range is rather significant, many players prefer to cast two or three [[Ice Strike|Ice Strikes]] instead.
history:
Prior to the [[Updates/12.55|Vocation Adjustments Update]], [[Druid|Druids]] could use either Ultimate Ice Strike '''OR''' [[Ultimate Terra Strike]]. If the player had learned both, they could switch between them by speaking to [[Zoltan]] and paying 500 [[gp]].
the spell's [[Cooldown#Cooldown_Groups|group cooldown]] was changed from 4 seconds to 2 seconds since [[Summer Update 2024]].
effect: [[File:Freezing Icon.gif]] Shoots a very powerful [[Ice Damage|ice]] missile at the selected target up to 3 square meters away. If no target is selected, the damage is focused on the single square right in front of the caster.
name: Ultimate Ice Strike

notes: This is a very handy [[spell]] and should be purchased as soon as possible. (The [[Stealth Ring]] produces the same effect.) Be aware certain [[Invisibility|creatures]] can "see invisibility," i.e. they are immune to it. [[Dark Magician]]s can cancel this spell by turning the player into a broom. This spell cannot make the caster invisible to players.
effect: Makes the caster invisible to some creatures. Duration is 3 minutes, 20 seconds.
name: Invisible

It will look like throwing a [[Blacksteel Sword]] when wielding a [[Sword Weapon|sword]], a [[Royal Axe]] when wielding an [[Axe Weapon|axe]] and a [[Cranial Basher]] when wielding a [[Club Weapon|club]].

name: Fire Wall

notes:
The final instant spell belonging to Druids. It poisoned the enemy with around 200 total damage, depending on your level.
Note that the target(s) will be poisoned, it won't make full damage when you attack. It was used a lot by druids when cleaning the [[Orc Fortress]], it was a good strategy if you went with a team, if you didn't, it was a waste. It was also excellent to use that spell in [[Nargor]] since [[Pirates]] did not have immunity to poison. If used against players, players could use the [[Antidote]] spell or the [[Antidote Rune]] to get rid of the [[poisoned|poison]] before major damage is done. Therefore, you should never buy this [[spell]] with sole purpose of [[Player Killing]]. This spell was replaced in the [[Winter Update 2007]] by [[Wrath of Nature]] and [[Eternal Winter]].
effect: [[File:Poisoned Icon.gif]] Poisons enemies around the caster.
name: Poison Storm

notes: One of the main offensive spells used by [[Paladin|paladins]]. Serves the same purpose as [[Ethereal Spear]], compared to which it costs 5 mana less and deals slightly more damage, but has a shorter range of only 4 squares.

Wound Cleansing was originally a premium only spell but was changed in the [[Winter Update 2010]] to reduce the level requirement and make it a free account spell; at the same time a stronger version of Wound Cleansing was introduced: [[Intense Wound Cleansing]]. 

Wound Cleansing replaced [[Light Healing]] for Knights although it is far more effective (much greater HP/Mana ratio) than its earlier counterpart.

notes:
This spell is basically useless against monsters because they usually cast Invisible again in few seconds, but if used against a player it may be useful as they might not have enough mana to cast it again. When you're playing on [[Hardcore PvP]], [[Open PvP#Retro Open PvP|Retro Open PvP]] or [[Retro Hardcore PvP]] game worlds, this spell may break the enemy's [[Stealth Ring|stealth ring]], if he's using one.

It has the same range as the [[Mass Healing]] spell.
